mouseTrapBeginnerGuide.title: Mouse Trap Beginner Guide
mouseTrapBeginnerGuide.intro: Mouse Trap is a turn-based digital board game for up to four players. Each match is a race to pick up, steal, and protect cheese before the Cheese Machine runs out.

steps.title: Choose a Game Mode
steps.description: Start with offline Single Player to learn against AI opponents, or choose Pass and Play, local console play, private online multiplayer, or open online multiplayer.
steps.tip: Single Player is the easiest place to learn the board prompts without waiting for other players.

steps.title: Choose Your Mouse
steps.description: Select a mouse color or fur and equip an available outfit. The classic red, blue, yellow, and green mice are available on supported versions.
steps.tip: Mouse appearance is cosmetic and does not change your match objective.

steps.title: Follow the Turn Prompts
steps.description: Players take turns moving around the animated board. Complete the action shown after movement, such as collecting cheese, producing cheese, stealing from another mouse, or starting a special event.
steps.tip: Check every on-screen choice before confirming because some turns can affect another player's cheese total.

steps.title: Collect and Hoard Cheese
steps.description: Build your match total whenever cheese becomes available. The winner is determined by the amount of cheese held when the match ends.
steps.tip: A small lead can disappear after a successful steal, so keep collecting even when you are ahead.

steps.title: Steal from Rival Mice
steps.description: Use stealing opportunities to take cheese from opponents and increase your own total. Targeting the current leader creates the largest possible score swing.
steps.tip: Avoid spreading steals evenly when one player has a clear lead.

steps.title: Use the Action Contraption
steps.description: Trigger the famous chain-reaction Mouse Trap when the game presents the opportunity. The animated sequence can interfere with an opponent and change the balance of the race.
steps.tip: The contraption can succeed or fail, so keep collecting cheese instead of relying only on the trap.

steps.title: Finish with the Most Cheese
steps.description: The match ends when the Cheese Machine runs out. The mouse holding the most cheese at that point wins the game.
steps.tip: Watch the remaining cheese supply and prioritize immediate gains as the end approaches.

steps.title: Spend Your Cheese Hoard
steps.description: After a completed game, your remaining cheese is added to your persistent cheese hoard. This hoard can be used to unlock additional outfits for your mouse.
steps.tip: Completing matches helps unlock cosmetics even when you do not win.

mouseTrapHowToPlay.title: How to Play Mouse Trap
mouseTrapHowToPlay.intro: The digital Mouse Trap game replaces the physical setup with a fully animated 3D board. Up to four mice compete to secure the most cheese while using steals and the chain-reaction contraption against their rivals.

faqs.question: What is the main objective in Mouse Trap?
faqs.answer: Collect, steal, and hold more cheese than every other player. When the Cheese Machine runs out, the mouse with the highest cheese total wins the match.

faqs.question: How many people can play Mouse Trap?
faqs.answer: Mouse Trap supports one to four players. Options include offline Single Player against AI, offline Pass and Play, supported local console multiplayer, private online games, and open online multiplayer.

faqs.question: How does a Mouse Trap match begin?
faqs.answer: Select a game mode, choose your mouse, and equip an outfit. Players then enter the animated board and take turns completing the movement and action prompts shown by the game.

faqs.question: How do turns work in Mouse Trap?
faqs.answer: Each mouse acts in turn and moves around the board. After movement, the game resolves or presents an action that can produce cheese, award cheese, steal cheese, activate the contraption, or begin another board event.

faqs.question: How is cheese collected in Mouse Trap?
faqs.answer: Cheese is gained through board actions and Cheese Machine opportunities. Your current match total is displayed throughout the game and determines your position in the race.

faqs.question: How does stealing work in Mouse Trap?
faqs.answer: Certain actions let a player take cheese from a rival. A successful steal lowers the opponent's total while increasing the stealing player's total, making it especially useful against the current leader.

faqs.question: What does the Mouse Trap contraption do?
faqs.answer: The action contraption starts the game's famous animated chain reaction. It is used to interfere with another mouse, although the sequence can succeed or fail before reaching its final stage.

faqs.question: What is the Riggs the cat event in Mouse Trap?
faqs.answer: Riggs the cat appears in a bonus game included in the digital edition. Follow the on-screen instructions when the event begins and complete its interactive sequence before returning to the main board.

faqs.question: When does a Mouse Trap game end?
faqs.answer: The race continues until the Cheese Machine runs out. Final cheese totals are then compared, and the player holding the most cheese is declared the winner.

faqs.question: What happens to cheese after a Mouse Trap match?
faqs.answer: The cheese held at the end of a completed game is added to your persistent cheese hoard. Saved cheese can be spent to unlock additional cosmetic outfits.

mouseTrapCheeseStrategy.title: Mouse Trap Cheese Strategy Guide
mouseTrapCheeseStrategy.intro: Winning Mouse Trap requires more than collecting every visible piece of cheese. Strong players watch the leaderboard, reduce the leading mouse's total, and shift priorities as the Cheese Machine approaches empty.

strategies.title: Track the Current Leader
strategies.description: Check each player's cheese total before choosing a target. Direct steals and disruptive actions toward the mouse with the largest pile.
strategies.bestWhen: One opponent begins building a clear lead.
strategies.result: The leader loses momentum while you create a larger relative gain.

strategies.title: Take Guaranteed Cheese
strategies.description: Prioritize immediate cheese gains when the alternative depends on an uncertain contraption result. Reliable collection keeps your score moving upward every round.
strategies.bestWhen: The Cheese Machine is running low or you are already close to first place.
strategies.result: You avoid ending the match with an unused scoring opportunity.

strategies.title: Use Steals for Score Swings
strategies.description: A steal both increases your total and reduces an opponent's total. This makes it more valuable than an equal-sized collection when taken from the player you need to overtake.
strategies.bestWhen: You can steal from first place or from a close rival.
strategies.result: The gap between you and the target changes in both directions.

strategies.title: Pressure the Leader with the Trap
strategies.description: Use contraption opportunities against the mouse whose cheese total creates the greatest threat. Avoid repeatedly targeting a player who is already far behind.
strategies.bestWhen: A rival has enough cheese to remain ahead after a normal collection turn.
strategies.result: The strongest opponent faces the greatest disruption.

strategies.title: Watch the Cheese Machine
strategies.description: The remaining Cheese Machine supply acts as the match timer. Shift from long-term disruption toward direct scoring as the available cheese approaches empty.
strategies.bestWhen: The game is entering its final turns.
strategies.result: You finish with points in hand instead of waiting for a later opportunity.

strategies.title: Protect a Narrow Lead
strategies.description: When you move into first place, continue adding cheese rather than assuming the lead is safe. Opponents can still reduce your total through steals and trap actions.
strategies.bestWhen: You lead by only a small amount.
strategies.result: Your advantage becomes harder to erase with one rival action.

strategies.title: Avoid Wasting Attacks
strategies.description: Target selection matters more than attacking as often as possible. Disrupting last place usually provides less value than reducing the total of a player competing for the win.
strategies.bestWhen: Several opponents are available as targets.
strategies.result: Each offensive action has a stronger effect on the final standings.

strategies.title: Complete Matches for Outfits
strategies.description: Cheese remaining at the end of a game contributes to your persistent cheese hoard, which unlocks cosmetic outfits. Finishing matches advances your collection even without a victory.
strategies.bestWhen: You are building your outfit collection.
strategies.result: Regular play produces long-term cosmetic progress.

mouseTrapMultiplayerGuide.title: Mouse Trap Multiplayer Guide
mouseTrapMultiplayerGuide.intro: Mouse Trap includes online and offline options for families, friends, and solo players. Exact local hardware and online subscription requirements depend on the platform being used.

modes.hardware: Each player uses a supported device or console with the game
modes.subscription: Nintendo Switch Online on Switch; PlayStation Plus on PS4; no console subscription on mobile
modes.bestFor: Playing remotely with invited friends and family

modes.hardware: Each player uses a supported device or console with the game
modes.subscription: Nintendo Switch Online on Switch; PlayStation Plus on PS4; no console subscription on mobile
modes.bestFor: Joining other Mouse Trap players when friends are unavailable

modes.connection: Internet not required for local play
modes.hardware: One compatible console and supported controllers; one controller is required per player
modes.subscription: No online membership required for offline local play
modes.bestFor: Playing together on one television or console

modes.hardware: One mobile device or supported system shared between players
modes.bestFor: Travel, shared-device sessions, and playing without extra controllers
Learning the rules, practicing strategy, or playing without other people

mouseTrapTrapChainReaction.title: Mouse Trap Chain Reaction Guide
mouseTrapTrapChainReaction.intro: The largest piles of cheese can appear beneath the trap, making this area both rewarding and dangerous. When a rival moves into the trap zone or begins building a strong lead, activating the contraption starts a long animated chain reaction that may catch the exposed mouse.

stages.title: Watch the Trap Area
stages.description: Large cheese rewards attract players beneath the trap. Track which opponent is entering the danger area and how much cheese they currently hold.
stages.strategy: Prioritize opponents who are leading the match or carrying a large amount of cheese.

stages.title: Choose the Right Moment
stages.description: Activate the trap when another mouse is exposed and slowing that player would meaningfully change the standings.
stages.strategy: Save the activation for a clear leader, a direct rival, or a late-game comeback before the Cheese Machine runs out.

stages.title: Swing the Boot
stages.description: The chain reaction begins when the boot swings and kicks the ball into motion.
stages.strategy: Once activated, the contraption runs automatically and players must wait for the full sequence to resolve.

stages.title: Follow the Ball
stages.description: The ball rolls through the machine's obstacles, travels through the air, and lands in the bathtub section of the contraption.
stages.strategy: The long animation creates suspense because the trap result is not settled immediately.

stages.title: Launch the Diver
stages.description: The moving mechanism reaches the springboard and launches the diver, completing the final part of the Rube Goldberg-style sequence.
stages.strategy: Use this moment to check the remaining cheese totals and plan your next collection or stealing move.

stages.title: Resolve the Trap
stages.description: The cage drops at the end of the chain reaction and determines whether the targeted mouse is caught.
stages.strategy: After a successful disruption, move quickly toward valuable cheese while the affected opponent recovers.

mouseTrapMiceOutfits.title: Mouse Trap Mice and Outfits Guide
mouseTrapMiceOutfits.intro: Mouse Trap lets every player choose a mouse and add an outfit before a match. Cheese remaining at the end of a game is transferred to the player's cheese hoard, where it can be spent to unlock additional cosmetic items.

items.title: Classic Mouse Colors
items.platform: Mobile, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation and Steam
items.description: The digital game includes the four classic player colors - red, blue, yellow, and green - used to distinguish mice during a match.

items.title: Fur Selection
items.description: The Nintendo Switch edition allows players to choose from an array of mouse colors and fur appearances before selecting an outfit.

items.title: Mobile Starter Wardrobe
items.description: The iOS edition includes 22 outfits with the base game. Additional cosmetic items can be unlocked with cheese or obtained through optional purchases.

items.title: Nintendo Switch Wardrobe
items.description: The Nintendo Switch edition lists 34 outfits as included with the game, providing a larger starting wardrobe than the mobile listing.

items.title: Cheese Hoard Unlocks
items.description: At the end of every game, the cheese held by the player is added to the cheese hoard and can be used to unlock new outfits.

items.title: Optional Mobile Cosmetics
items.description: The premium mobile game is ad-free but offers optional cosmetic items that can be purchased with real money.

mouseTrapGameModes.title: Mouse Trap Game Modes Guide
mouseTrapGameModes.intro: The digital game provides four main ways to play. Offline modes are suitable for travel and solo practice, while online modes connect friends or match players with Mouse Trap fans around the world.

modes.name: Single Player
modes.bestFor: Learning the board, testing strategies, and playing while traveling

modes.name: Private Online Multiplayer
modes.bestFor: Remote family game nights and planned matches with friends

modes.name: Open Online Multiplayer
modes.bestFor: Finding opponents when friends are unavailable

modes.name: Pass and Play
modes.opponents: Friends and family in the same room
modes.bestFor: Road trips, family gatherings, and local play without separate devices

mouseTrapPlatformsReleaseDates.title: Mouse Trap Platforms and Release Dates
mouseTrapPlatformsReleaseDates.intro: Mouse Trap first launched on mobile in 2023 before arriving on Nintendo Switch and PlayStation 4. A Windows version is listed on Steam with single-player, online PvP, shared-screen multiplayer, and Remote Play Together support.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Mouse Trap - the digital chain-reaction board game

questions.question: What is Mouse Trap?
questions.answer: Mouse Trap is a turn-based digital board game for one to four players. You collect, steal, and protect cheese on an animated 3D board, and the player with the most cheese when the Cheese Machine runs out wins.

questions.question: How many players does Mouse Trap support?
questions.answer: Mouse Trap supports one to four players through Single Player against AI, online private and open matches, local console multiplayer, and offline Pass and Play.

questions.question: What does the Mouse Trap contraption do?
questions.answer: The action contraption starts Mouse Trap's famous animated chain reaction. Trigger it at the right moment to interfere with a rival mouse, though the sequence can succeed or fail before the cage drops.

questions.question: Which platforms is Mouse Trap available on?
questions.answer: Mouse Trap is available on iOS, Android, Nintendo Switch, and PlayStation 4. A Windows PC version is listed on Steam as coming soon with Remote Play Together support.

questions.question: How do you unlock outfits in Mouse Trap?
questions.answer: Cheese held at the end of a completed Mouse Trap match is added to your persistent cheese hoard. Spend hoarded cheese to unlock additional cosmetic outfits for your mouse.

questions.question: Does Mouse Trap need an internet connection?
questions.answer: Only online multiplayer modes need an internet connection and, on console, the platform's online membership. Single Player and Pass and Play in Mouse Trap work fully offline.

cta.title: Ready to Play Mouse Trap?
cta.description: Download Mouse Trap on mobile and console, trigger the chain-reaction trap with up to four players, and join the official community for tips and updates.

guide.whatIsDescription: Our Mouse Trap guides walk you through every part of the game, from your first board setup to advanced trap-timing tactics. Each guide breaks the rules into clear steps so new players can start playing in minutes and veterans can sharpen their strategy.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand turns, moves, cheese collection, and how the trap activates so you always know what to do next.
whySteps.step2Description: Follow illustrated setup steps that get the board and chain-reaction contraption ready the right way, every time.
whySteps.step3Description: Apply proven tactics for trap timing and cheese stealing that work across the original and modern editions.

rules.whatIs: What Are the Mouse Trap Rules?
rules.whatIsDescription: The rules define how Mouse Trap is played: how mice move around the board, how cheese is collected and stolen, when the famous chain-reaction trap fires, and how a player wins. We explain every rule in plain language so nothing slows down your game night.

whySteps.step1Description: Know exactly how turns, moves, and cheese work so there are no arguments or confusion mid-game.
whySteps.step2Title: Trigger the Trap the Right Way
whySteps.step2Description: Understand the exact conditions that launch the chain reaction so the trap fires fairly every time.
whySteps.step3Description: Learn the victory conditions and player-count rules so everyone knows how the game is won.

setup.title: Mouse Trap Setup
setup.description: Complete Mouse Trap setup guides: board assembly, trap construction, piece placement, and chain-reaction build order.
setup.metaTitle: Mouse Trap Setup - Board, Trap & Pieces Assembly
setup.metaDescription: Set up Mouse Trap correctly: build the board, assemble the chain-reaction trap, place the pieces, and start playing fast.
setup.whatIsDescription: Setup is everything you do before the first turn: building the board, constructing the chain-reaction trap contraption piece by piece, and placing the mice and cheese. A correct setup makes the game run smoothly and the trap fire without jamming.

whySteps.step1Description: Assemble the chain-reaction contraption in the right order so every gear, staircase, and cage works as intended.
whySteps.step2Description: Lay out the board, position the mice, and stage the cheese so the game is ready to start.
whySteps.step3Description: Sidestep the frequent assembly mistakes that make the trap jam or misfire during play.

strategy.title: Mouse Trap Strategy
strategy.description: Winning Mouse Trap strategy: cheese stealing, trap timing, comeback tactics, and family-friendly tips for every match.
strategy.metaTitle: Mouse Trap Strategy - Win Tips & Cheese Tactics
strategy.metaDescription: Dominate Mouse Trap with proven strategy: when to steal cheese, perfect trap timing, comeback plans, and family tips.
strategy.whatIsDescription: Strategy is how you turn the rules into wins: deciding when to race for cheese, when to bait opponents into the trap, and how to recover when you fall behind. Good strategy keeps every game close, exciting, and winnable.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn when to lure rivals onto the trap space so the chain reaction catches them at the worst moment.
whySteps.step2Description: Pick the right moments to steal cheese and protect your stash so you stay ahead on the scoreboard.
whySteps.step3Description: Use proven comeback tactics to climb back into the game even when you are trailing early.
